evals.prompt: What is the full domain architecture of EGFR (UniProt P00533)? I want each InterPro domain and where it sits on the sequence.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-interpro. Calls the InterPro REST API (protein/UniProt/P00533/) to retrieve all matching entries with their entry_protein_locations, and reports the ordered domain architecture with start-end coordinates on the sequence.

assertions.value: Returns the ordered domain architecture with sequence positions from the InterPro protein endpoint.

evals.prompt: I have a poorly characterized protein mapped to UniProt Q9Y4K3. Predict its likely function by pulling its InterPro family/domain annotations and the associated GO terms.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-interpro. Retrieves the protein's InterPro entries (grouping families, domains, homologous superfamilies) and reads the associated GO terms (identifier, name, category code F/P/C) from metadata.go_terms, using them to infer likely function (e.g. ubiquitin-protein transferase activity, GO:0004842).

assertions.value: Reads GO terms from the InterPro protein/entry metadata (not a separate GO database) and uses the protein's families/domains to infer molecular function.

evals.prompt: Find every UniProt protein annotated with the protein kinase domain InterPro entry IPR000719 — I want the count and the members.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-interpro. Queries entry/InterPro/IPR000719/protein/UniProt/ to list proteins carrying that entry, reporting the total count and members (with pagination for large result sets).

assertions.value: Uses the InterPro entry-to-protein endpoint to enumerate members of the family/domain and reports the count.

evals.prompt: Look up the Pfam WW domain entry PF00397 and the InterPro entry it maps to (IPR001202), and tell me what type of signature it is.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-interpro. Fetches the entry via entry/Pfam/PF00397/ and entry/InterPro/IPR001202/, reporting the entry name and type (e.g. domain), noting Pfam is one of InterPro's member databases.

assertions.value: Resolves a Pfam member-database accession through the InterPro API and reports the entry type/name.

evals.prompt: Just give me the reviewed Swiss-Prot FASTA sequence and the organism for UniProt P00533 — only the entry record, no domain analysis.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-uniprot. The user wants the raw protein entry/FASTA from UniProt, not InterPro family/domain classification or signature annotations.

evals.prompt: Download the AlphaFold predicted 3D structure and pLDDT confidence for P00533 so I can inspect the folded domains in 3D.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-alphafold-db. The user wants a 3D structural model with confidence metrics, not sequence-based InterPro domain/family signature annotation.
